classObjective: Describe translations, dilations, and reflections of absolute value functions from their equations and graph absolute value functions.
workedExamples: 4-7, Example 1 — Describe Translations of Absolute Value Functions from Equations
4-7, Example 2 — Write the Equation from a Graph
4-7, Example 3 — Describe Dilations of Absolute Value Functions
4-7, Example 5 — Graph Absolute Value Functions and State Domain and Range
warmUp: What is the basic shape of an absolute value function's graph?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: vertex location (h, k); V-shape graphing; slope of rays; transformation parameter effects
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice describing transformations of absolute value functions and graphing with all key features
independentPractice: Practice problems describing and graphing absolute value functions including domain and range
assessment: Exit ticket: graph y = |x - 2| + 3 and state the vertex and intercepts
capReflection: What determines the position of the vertex in an absolute value function's graph?
